words.w: Right now, a volcano is erupting in Alaska.
Great Sitkin has oozed lava for months.
It sits on a chain of dozens of volcanoes.
Nobody can stand on them and watch.
So Alaska listens instead.
Every rumble sends sound through the rock,
and pressure through the air.
The Alaska Volcano Observatory turns that sound
into a picture, a spectrogram.
Then they trained a model to read it,
on over two hundred seventy thousand pictures.
It tells tremor from explosion, quake from noise.
On its test, right about eighty seven percent.
But hearing is not foreseeing.
It can name the sound the mountain makes.
It cannot tell you the hour it blows.
Push it past what it learned, and it slips.
A person still keeps watch.
So the mountain keeps speaking,
and Alaska keeps listening.
